bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, and the opponent’s first offer gives me the minimum utility on every issue. I am targeting my maximum-utility bid while establishing my preferred anchor, especially on the high-weight issues issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (about 0.98) because it is still the opening phase at t=0.02 and there is no time pressure to make major concessions. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueE offer, which is very low utility for me and shows no concession yet, so I will only make a small move on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the most important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has moved from an all-valueE offer to concessions on issueA and issueB, but they are still holding issueC/D/E low for me, so I will concede only on my lower-weight issues while protecting issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.94 because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is little need to concede on my most important issues yet. The opponent has clearly improved their offers to me from all valueE to a bid worth about 0.62, especially conceding on issueA and issueB, so I will hold firm on issueA/issueB/issueC while making a small concession on a low-weight issue and avoiding repeating my exact previous bid.

I targeted a high utility around 0.88 because it is still very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has improved from very low offers to bids worth about 0.62 to me, but they still push issueD and issueE to valueE, so I concede mainly on those lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.84, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueD and issueE while holding firm on the high-weight issueA and issueB. At t=0.10 it is still early, so I do not need to drop all the way to the opponent’s last bid, but their offers show meaningful concession toward valueA/valueB on my important issues, so I am making a reciprocal moderate move.
